zh.Great Bromley, Essex, England: 英國艾可格亞特 布羅姆利
zh.lifeSummary: 當David Stone於大約1540年出生在英國艾可格亞特 布羅姆利時，他的父親Simon Stone34歲，母親Agnes Speed30歲。他於1566年7月10日與Elizabeth Hewitt在英國艾可格亞特 布羅姆利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1625年4月25日在他的家鄉享年86歲。

zh-hans.Great Bromley, Essex, England: 英国艾可格亞特 布羅姆利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当David Stone于大约1540年出生在英国艾可格亞特 布羅姆利时，他的父亲Simon Stone34岁，母亲Agnes Speed30岁。他于1566年7月10日与Elizabeth Hewitt在英国艾可格亞特 布羅姆利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1625年4月25日在他的家乡享年86岁。

nameMeanings.name: Stone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

variants.name: Stoner
meanings: English (Sussex): topographic name from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone’ + -er, used for someone who lived by a prominent rock or stone, or perhaps who came from a place called Stone, although this formation is more rare. See Stone and compare Stoneman .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Steiner  .

variants.name: Stones
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of Stone with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. A number of the places called Stone are sometimes recorded with plural spellings in the medieval period, including Stone (Staffordshire), recorded as Stanes in 1187.

variants.name: Stenner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt.

German: occupational name for a stonemason or stonecutter, or a topographic name for someone who lived on stony ground, from a derivative of Middle High German stein ‘stone’, ‘stony place’. Compare Stone and Stein .

variants.name: Clougherty
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Clochartaigh ‘descendant of Clochartach’, a personal name, probably formed with cloch ‘stone’, Stone being an alternative Anglicized form of the surname.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Stoneman
meanings: English: perhaps an occupational name for someone who was the servant (Middle English man) of a person surnamed Stone . Alternatively, the name may have been an alias of Stone , used for someone who lived by a prominent stone or rock or at a minor place so named (Middle English ston + man).
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name for someone who worked with stone or who sold precious stones; a jeweler.

English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Ston(e)man (Old English Stān(e)mann), a pet form of Old English personal names beginning with Stān- (from the word ‘stone’, ‘rock’) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -mann.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When David Stone was born about 1540, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, his father, Simon Stone, was 34 and his mother, Agnes Speed, was 30. He married Elizabeth Hewitt on 10 July 1566, in Great Bromley, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 25 April 1625, in his hometown, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque David Stone est né environ 1540, à Great Bromley, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Simon Stone, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Agnes Speed, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Hewitt le 10 juillet 1566, à Great Bromley, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 25 avril 1625, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als David Stone um 1540 in Great Bromley, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Simon Stone 34 und seine Mutter Agnes Speed 30. Er hat am 10. Juli 1566 in Great Bromley, Essex, England Elizabeth Hewitt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 25. April 1625 im Alter von 86 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando David Stone è nato il circa 1540 nel Great Bromley, Essex, England, il suo padre, Simon Stone, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Speed, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Hewitt il 10 luglio 1566, nel Great Bromley, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 25 aprile 1625, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 86 anni.

ja.Great Bromley, Essex, England: イギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリー
ja.lifeSummary: David Stoneが約1540年にイギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリーで生まれた時，父Simon Stoneは34歳，母Agnes Speedは30歳であった。1566年7月10日にイギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリーでElizabeth Hewittと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1625年4月25日に故郷で86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: David Stone(이)가 대략 1540년 영국 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon Stone(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Speed(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1566년 7월 10일 영국 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 Elizabeth Hewitt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1625년 4월 25일 그의 고향에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando David Stone nasceu aproximadamente 1540, em Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Simon Stone, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Speed, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Hewitt em 10 de julho de 1566, em Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de abril de 1625, em sua cidade natal, com 86 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда David Stone родился около 1540 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Simon Stone было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Speed, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Hewitt 10 июля 1566 г. в местности грит бромли, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 25 апреля 1625 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 86 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando David Stone nació aproximadamente 1540, en Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Simon Stone, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Agnes Speed, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Hewitt el 10 de julio de 1566, en Great Bromley, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 25 de abril de 1625, en su pueblo, a la edad de 86 años.
